name: exploratory_stall_no_artifact
description: The agent spent the run inspecting files, grepping, reading docs/source, diagnosing, or planning, but never created the required deliverable file, script, config, query, binary, or output. A concrete improvement would be to force an early minimal artifact and iterate on it rather than remaining in reconnaissance mode.

name: unrecovered_tooling_or_external_blocker
description: The agent hit missing tools, build dependencies, unavailable compilers, package-install problems, network/API/video access failures, or other environmental blockers and did not switch to a viable fallback. This calls for better contingency behavior: use installed alternatives, vendor small replacements, adjust the approach, or continue from cached/local data instead of stopping at setup/access failures.

name: partial_workflow_not_finalized
description: The agent began a plausible solution but failed to complete the required workflow: it did not rerun after discovering the fix, left validation failures unresolved, failed to execute a generated script, or left a merge/conflict/task state unfinished. The fix is to require end-to-end completion checks against the task’s stated success condition before stopping.

name: flawed_or_non_general_solution
description: The agent produced code or output, but the core method was wrong, brittle, overfit to visible examples, violated the specified interface/format, used a narrow regex/heuristic, or ignored required semantics. A better agent should reason from the full specification, test hidden-case-like behavior, and avoid shortcuts that only satisfy visible examples or superficial checks.

name: deployment_configuration_incomplete
description: The agent installed, launched, or partially configured a service/runtime but missed a critical persistent configuration detail or did not verify the actual external contract, such as root SSH login, PATH availability, VM desktop accessibility, or web/service reachability. The improvement is to perform end-to-end verification from the grader’s perspective, not just local process/status checks.

name: execution_control_or_protocol_failure
description: The failure was caused by losing control of the terminal/process or not following the benchmark interaction protocol: runaway foreground jobs, Vim/interactive automation mistakes, or failing to submit the required final completion JSON. Agents need stronger process management, timeout/interrupt handling, and protocol-state awareness before declaring completion.
